I received one box of "Besli 1.2 Gallon Drawstring Trash Bags". There are 120 trash bags in the box.

The box is like the dispenser it has a flap that opens up and you just pull the trash bags out of it. The trash bags come out of the box sideways, they are attached to each other by the sides of the trash bags. They are super easy to tear apart / separate.

The actual trash bags are really an impressive quality. They are White in color with a Black drawstring. They are a nice thickness, super easy to open & close. No scent at all.

Well made, they don't rip or tear easily when they are full of trash. They have a functional well made drawstring that can be used to tie up the bag as well as carry the full trash bag by. The drawstring doesn't come unattached from the rest of the trash bag either when pulled on.

These trash bags were the perfect size & fit for the trash can I have in my bedroom beside my bed.

I would actually purchase these trash bags at the listed price of $11.99. They are really worth it!

Despite being super small in size (just 1.2 gallon) their quality is much better then many of the 30 gallon size trash bags I have purchased before. Which is saying something because the 30 gallon trash bags were all super popular named brand trash bags.

I would totally recommend this item.
top_profane.title: Excellent Quality! 🏆

With inflation the highest since the 1980s, like any sane person who is trying to save money buying LACTAID milk, after reading reviews, and doing calculations (as I'm a math and science wiz), I bought these two items to test out and perform the math for you all.

My lactose BACKGROUND: In grade school through high school, I was able to drink milk just fine. Halfway through college, I started noticing bloating, gas, stomach cramps, and diarrhea after drinking milk. Fast forward a few decades and I can generally tolerate cheese and pizza, with occasional minor diarrhea a few hours later if I eat too much dairy. However, no cramps etc. If I drink milk though, even a few sips, or especially half a glass, I'll get painful cramps, and bad diarrhea. So I consider myself moderately lactose intolerant. My body or my gut bacteria must produce some minimal amount of lactase enzyme, otherwise I wouldn't be able to tolerate cheese.

===========================
*** ITEM: B007VTSMWG (Lactase Enzyme Lacteeze Drops 15.5 ml Liquid by Gelda Sci)

This item has 1,250 lactase units (LU) (or sometimes abbreviated FCC ALU or just ALU) per 5 drops, or 250 LU per 1 drop.

Cost: $19.50, or 6.29 cents per drop/250 LU.

It comes in 15.5 mL per bottle, so 15.5 mL x 20 drops/mL= 310 drops, therefore $19.50/310 drops = 6.29 cents per drop, or 6.29
top_rant.title: Lowest price per drop/lactase unit by an order of magnitude AND it works!

These 7 gallon trash bags are the perfect size for the small trash cans we have throughout the house in the bathrooms, bedrooms, and in my home office. They're thin enough to be nearly translucent, but they're strong enough that I can't poke a finger through the sides of the plastic without really working at it, so they're durable enough to hold the trash without tearing.

These come as 6 colorful rolls of 20 bags each, for a total of 120 7 gallon bags at an economical price. These bags are flat across the top and perforated. We use [[ASIN:B000S0ZQCE Rubbermaid Commercial Plastic 7-Gallon Trash Can, Beige]] for our small trash cans, and these bags fit them just about right, leaving just enough extra to fold down over the sides and tie to the can so the bag doesn't slide down when you toss trash in.

These are practical, economical, and the colors are much more interesting than the standard white or black trash bags I usually find at the store. There are lightweight yet durable and economical enough to be a smart move for a small office or for family needs. I'm happy with the product and can't find any negatives.

I received these bags for evaluation and review from the manufacturer.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Excellent quality small garbage bags

top_five_star_obscene.text: As long as you know what to expect going in, you should be pleased with these biodegradable small garbage bags. They are thin but fairly strong. They will stand up to some stretching, but aren’t going to handle anything sharp. I’ve used many biodegradable garbage bags and like all the others, these will start to break down within a week if you put anything wet in them. We have larger ones we use in the kitchen garbage, and while we compost most of our food waste, sometimes something wet gets in the bag. Since it’s not uncommon for the same bag to be in the garbage can for a week and not emptied until trash pickup day, it’s also not uncommon for some leakage by the end of the week. The same is the case with these. They are better suited for dry garbage, or as indicated as their purpose, for compostables that you will empty frequently. That said, I’m willing to put up with some inconvenience in order to keep plastic garbage bag use at a minimum in our house, so I’m happy with these as they help achieve that goal.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Good quality, but know what to expect!

These HEFTY ULTRA STRONG TALL KITCHEN DRAWSTRING TRASH BAGS hold large amounts of kitchen garbage. They are very strong--definitely strong enough to handle the weight of the wet garbage that will fit in the bags. The drawstring closes the bag tightly enough to keep most garbage odors inside the bag once the bag goes out to the garbage can.

I like the "Lavender & Sweet Vanilla" fragrance, but there was a time when I didn’t like lavender, so this fragrance may not be for everybody. It does seem to neutralize garbage odors somewhat. My favorite option for kitchen and laundry supplies is still “no scent”, because many perfumes and commercial fragrances make me sneeze.

The drawstrings are an extra that I can do without, because I haven’t noticed that my non-drawstring tall kitchen bags are falling down into the trash can. However, if you have this problem, the drawstrings will help secure the bag at the top.

I really like these black kitchen bags! First, our kitchen trash can MUST (by design of kitchen space) be placed as the first item you see entering the kitchen. As you may imagine, a white trash bag tucked of the edge of the can doesn't look too great, especially one with a bright colored (like red) tie. The black bags just "disappear," and you only see the trash can.

About privacy: I know it may seem silly--why would you care what people see in your trash can (through the white translucent bags), as long as you are not participating in felony-level activities? Well, I care. Our vacation home is in a multi-dwelling building, and I'd just as soon keep my private life, including what I purchase, to myself. And have have heard a few comments from other residents about what they've seen in the outdoor community trash cans. And yes, occasionally I want to discard recyclables in the trash, for a number of reasons. So, you don't care? Well, then this item may not be for you.

I'm glad these black trash bags are on Amazon, as the local grocery store no longer carries them.
